goal: Build a dual-robot dynamic fixture hold canonical: R1 holds a workpiece (Cube_workpiece) mid-air at a fixed transfer pose while R2 picks a mating part (Cube_mating) and stacks it onto the held workpiece. The hardest collision-avoidance case in the research set — both robots simultaneously occupy the central workspace, and R2 must treat R1's hand as a moving obstacle. Realizes Industrial Set 1 #4 Dual-Robot Dynamic Fixture Hold.
thoughts:
Six dual-robot fixture-hold specifics for CP-76:
1) Two Frankas: R1 (FixtureHolder) on -Y side at (0, -0.5, 0.75), R2 (Inserter) on +Y side at (0, 0.5, 0.75) — face-to-face across the central work zone.
2) Cube_workpiece spawned on R1's feeder conveyor; R1 picks and holds it static at the central transfer pose [0, 0, 0.95] (mid-air, 20cm above table).
3) Cube_mating spawned on R2's feeder conveyor; R2 picks and stacks on top of the held workpiece — drop_target = transfer pose + cube height = [0, 0, 1.00].
4) FixtureHold extension: drop_target IS the held position. Once R1 places at [0, 0, 0.95] there's nothing physically supporting the cube — it would fall. Mitigation: solid pedestal at the transfer pose (small Cube prim with collision) catches the workpiece for R1's hand to 'hold against'.
5) R2 uses register_moving_obstacle to track R1's panda_hand as a runtime obstacle so cuRobo plans around the holding arm. Runtime polling is Sprint 4+, but the registration metadata is set at canonical-time.
6) The two robots run independently — R1 cycles workpiece → pedestal (acts as 'hold'), R2 cycles mating → on top. Realizes #4 dual-robot dynamic fixture hold in build form.
code:
create_prim(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", prim_type="DomeLight")
set_attribute(prim_path="/World/DomeLight", attr_name="inputs:intensity", value=1000.0)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Ground", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, -0.5], scale=[20, 20, 1])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Ground", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cell", prim_type="Xform")
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Table", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.375], scale=[1.5, 0.8, 0.375])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Table", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

set_physics_scene_config(config={"enable_gpu_dynamics": False, "broadphase_type": "MBP"})

# R1 (FixtureHolder) on -Y side
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/FixtureHolder",
    position=[0, -0.5, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, 0.7071068],
)
# R2 (Inserter) on +Y side, facing R1
robot_wizard(
    robot_name="franka_panda",
    dest_path="/World/Inserter",
    position=[0, 0.5, 0.75],
    orientation=[0.7071068, 0, 0, -0.7071068],
)

# R1's feeder conveyor — workpiece flows in from -X
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/HolderConveyor",
    position=[-0.6, -0.4, 0.78],
    size=[1.5, 0.3, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[0.2, 0, 0],
)

# R2's feeder conveyor — mating part flows in from +X
create_conveyor(
    prim_path="/World/InserterConveyor",
    position=[0.6, 0.4, 0.78],
    size=[1.5, 0.3, 0.05],
    surface_velocity=[-0.2, 0, 0],
)

# Workpiece (R1's cube)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", prim_type="Cube", position=[-1.0, -0.4, 0.835], size=0.06)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", schema_name=api)

# Mating part (R2's cube)
create_prim(prim_path="/World/Cube_mating", prim_type="Cube", position=[1.0, 0.4, 0.835], size=0.05)
for api in ("PhysicsRigidBodyAPI", "PhysicsCollisionAPI", "PhysicsMassAPI", "PhysxRigidBodyAPI"):
    apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/Cube_mating", schema_name=api)

bulk_set_attribute(prim_paths=["/World/Cube_workpiece", "/World/Cube_mating"], attr="physxRigidBody:sleepThreshold", value=0.0)
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube_workpiece", material_name="rubber")
apply_physics_material(prim_path="/World/Cube_mating", material_name="rubber")

# Hold pedestal — small cube at transfer pose; R1 places workpiece on top
create_prim(prim_path="/World/HoldPedestal", prim_type="Cube", position=[0, 0, 0.85], scale=[0.04, 0.04, 0.05])
apply_api_schema(prim_path="/World/HoldPedestal", schema_name="PhysicsCollisionAPI")

# Pick sensors
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/HolderSensor", position=[-0.2, -0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])
add_proximity_sensor(sensor_path="/World/InserterSensor", position=[0.2, 0.4, 0.835], size=[0.06, 0.06, 0.06])

# Register R1's panda_hand as moving obstacle for R2's cuRobo planner.
# Runtime obstacle polling is Sprint 4+; canonical-time call sets the
# curobo:moving_obstacles attr on /World/Inserter.
register_moving_obstacle(
    robot_path="/World/Inserter",
    obstacle_path="/World/FixtureHolder/panda_hand",
)

# R1 controller — picks workpiece, places on HoldPedestal
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/FixtureHolder",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/HolderSensor",
    belt_path="/World/HolderConveyor",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_workpiece"],
    destination_path="/World/HoldPedestal",
    drop_target=[0, 0, 0.92],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/HolderConveyor", "/World/InserterConveyor", "/World/HoldPedestal"],
)

# R2 controller — picks mating part, stacks on top of workpiece on pedestal
setup_pick_place_controller(
    robot_path="/World/Inserter",
    target_source="curobo",
    sensor_path="/World/InserterSensor",
    belt_path="/World/InserterConveyor",
    source_paths=["/World/Cube_mating"],
    destination_path="/World/HoldPedestal",
    drop_target=[0, 0, 0.99],
    planning_obstacles=["/World/Table", "/World/HolderConveyor", "/World/InserterConveyor", "/World/HoldPedestal"],
)
extension_notes: CP-76 extends CP-68's robot-to-robot handoff with moving obstacle pattern. Both robots place AT the same destination (HoldPedestal) — R1 first, R2 stacks on top. The pedestal substitutes for actual mid-air holding (which would need runtime FixedJoint between R1 hand and workpiece). Realizes Industrial Set 1 #4 in build-spec form. Runtime mid-air-hold + collision-aware coordination is Sprint 4+ work.
failure_modes: Both robots try to access HoldPedestal simultaneously — no mutex. Sprint 4+ needs setup_robot_claim_mutex on the pedestal prim.
drop_target precision: cuRobo's ~17cm drop precision means R2 may miss R1's workpiece by 5-10cm even with correct logic. Realistic stacking needs sensor_gated controller or visual servoing.
register_moving_obstacle is metadata-only at canonical time — cuRobo handler doesn't yet poll the obstacle list each tick. Runtime moving-obstacle integration is the next Sprint.
Same multi-robot Kit RPC limit as CP-51/CP-65 — single-tenant, can't run two parallel direct_eval against the same scene.
HoldPedestal collision via PhysicsCollisionAPI alone (no RigidBody) — static, won't move. Workpiece falls to it via gravity if drop is approximate.
verified_status: stable_fail; passes=0 n=22 wilson_lower=0.0; was form-gate-pending; demoted 2026-05-15

migration_deferred.details: Dual-robot dynamic fixture hold; fixture-hold role not yet defined; zero QA runs
